content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and an array AAA of size NNN, A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã.

We define a process as shown below, your task is to find the expected value of ansansans at the end of it.

1)1) 1) Choose a random number xxx, equiprobably in range [1,N][1, N][1,N]. Initialize ans=Ax,l=x,r=x.ans = A_x, l = x, r = x.ans=Ax‚Äã,l=x,r=x.

2)2) 2) Repeat the following N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 times :


define a temporary variable bbb as follows :

If l=1l = 1l=1, set b=1b = 1b=1;
else if r=nr = nr=n set b=0b = 0b=0;
else set b=0b = 0b=0 or 111 with equal probability.


If b=0b = 0b=0, first set l=l‚àí1l = l - 1l=l‚àí1, then set ans=ans+Al‚ãÖ(r‚àíl+1)ans = ans + A_l \cdot (r - l + 1)ans=ans+Al‚Äã‚ãÖ(r‚àíl+1).
If b=1b = 1b=1, first set r=r+1r = r + 1r=r+1, then set ans=ans+Ar‚ãÖ(r‚àíl+1)ans = ans + A_r \cdot (r - l + 1)ans=ans+Ar‚Äã‚ãÖ(r‚àíl+1).
It can be shown by the end of the process l=1l = 1l=1 and r=Nr = Nr=N.

Print the expected value of ansansans under modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Formally, it can be proven that the value can be written as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äã, where P,QP, QP,Q are integers and relatively prime and Qmod‚Äâ‚Äâ(109+7)‚âÝ0Q \mod (10^9 + 7) \ne 0Qmod(109+7)ÓÄÝ=0. Then, you need to print the unique integer RRR in range [0,109+7)[0, 10^9 + 7)[0,109+7) such that Q‚ãÖR‚â°PQ \cdot R \equiv PQ‚ãÖR‚â°P (mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,....,ANA_1, A_2, ...., A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,....,AN‚Äã representing the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected value of the variable ansansans.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

ans=ans+A2‚ãÖ2=5,l=1,r=2ans = ans + A_2 \cdot 2 = 5, l = 1, r = 2ans=ans+A2‚Äã‚ãÖ2=5,l=1,r=2. The process terminates.


x=2x = 2x=2 : This gives ans=2+2‚ãÖ1=4ans = 2 + 2 \cdot 1 = 4ans=2+2‚ãÖ1=4.

Since both of these are equiprobable, the expected value of ans=ans = ans= 4+52=92\frac{4 + 5}{2} = \frac{9}{2}24+5‚Äã=29‚Äã.You can verify 2‚ãÖ500000008‚â°92 \cdot 500000008 \equiv 92‚ãÖ500000008‚â°9 (mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7).
